text: The video shows the placement of three trocars for a robotic right hemicolectomy on a 51-year-old male patient with ascending colon cancer, highlighting the enhanced robotic platform used in the procedure.

The video shows the placement of three trocars for a robotic right hemicolectomy using a novel robotic platform. The trocars are positioned to facilitate access to the ascending colon in a 51-year-old male patient diagnosed with colon cancer.

The surgeon begins mobilization from the lateral side using a lateral to medial approach. The appendix is mobilized first, followed by the ascending colon. The hepatic flexure is then mobilized, and finally, the proximal transverse colon is mobilized.

The surgeon begins mobilization from the lateral side using a lateral to medial approach, initially focusing on the appendix. Subsequently, the ascending colon is mobilized, with visible manipulation of these structures.
The surgeon mobilizes the hepatic flexure, followed by the mobilization of the proximal transverse colon using a robotic platform.

The surgeon extracts the specimen through the transumbilical incision. The terminal ileum is transected, followed by ligation of the right branch of the middle colic artery and the ileocolic artery. The transverse colon is then transected, and a side-to-side two-layer anastomosis is performed.
